definition: The process of recording, summarizing, and reporting a company's financial transactions.
example: The accounting department closes the books at the end of every quarter.
exampleVi: Bộ phận kế toán chốt sổ sách vào cuối mỗi quý.
collocations: accounting department, accounting software, accounting practices, accounting period

definition: A professional who prepares, examines, and manages financial records for a person or company.
example: Our accountant reviewed the invoices before they were sent to the client.
exampleVi: Kế toán viên của chúng tôi đã kiểm tra các hóa đơn trước khi gửi cho khách hàng.
collocations: chartered accountant, hire an accountant, certified public accountant

definition: A book for keeping notes, especially one for keeping accounting records; a record book, a register.
example: Every transaction must be recorded in the general ledger before the monthly report is prepared.
exampleVi: Mọi giao dịch phải được ghi vào sổ cái trước khi lập báo cáo hàng tháng.
collocations: general ledger, ledger entry, update the ledger, ledger account

definition: The amount of money remaining in an account after deposits and withdrawals.
example: Please check your account balance before making the transfer.
exampleVi: Vui lòng kiểm tra số dư tài khoản trước khi thực hiện chuyển khoản.
collocations: account balance, balance sheet, check the balance, outstanding balance

definition: A document, especially a financial report such as a balance sheet, presented on a single page.
example: The accountant printed out the balance sheet for the board meeting.
exampleVi: Kế toán viên đã in bảng cân đối kế toán cho cuộc họp hội đồng quản trị.
collocations: balance sheet, spreadsheet, fact sheet, sheet of paper

definition: Something or someone of any value; any portion of one's property or effects so considered.
example: These shares are a valuable asset.
exampleVi: Những cổ phiếu này là tài sản có giá trị.
collocations: asset process, key asset

definition: An obligation, debt or responsibility owed to someone.
example: The company's total liabilities increased after it took out a new loan.
exampleVi: Tổng nợ phải trả của công ty tăng lên sau khi vay khoản vay mới.
collocations: current liability, long-term liability, liability insurance, limit liability

definition: The value of a company's assets after all liabilities have been subtracted; ownership interest in a company.
example: Shareholders' equity is listed at the bottom of the balance sheet.
exampleVi: Vốn chủ sở hữu của cổ đông được ghi ở cuối bảng cân đối kế toán.
collocations: shareholders' equity, equity investment, private equity, home equity

definition: A spending or consuming, often a disbursement of funds.
example: Buying the car was a big expense, but will be worth it in the long run.
exampleVi: Mua một chiếc ô tô là một khoản chi phí lớn nhưng sẽ có giá trị về lâu dài.
collocations: expense process, key expense

definition: The income returned by an investment.
example: The company's revenue grew by 15% compared to last year.
exampleVi: Doanh thu của công ty tăng 15% so với năm ngoái.
collocations: annual revenue, revenue growth, generate revenue, revenue stream

definition: The act of conducting or carrying out (business, negotiations, plans).
example: The transaction was made on Friday with the supplier.
exampleVi: Giao dịch được thực hiện vào thứ Sáu với nhà cung cấp.
collocations: financial transaction, process a transaction, transaction fee, business transaction

definition: A bill; a commercial document issued by a seller to a buyer indicating the products, quantities and agreed prices for products or services that the seller has already provided the buyer with. An invoice indicates that, unless paid in advance, payment is due by the buyer to the seller, according to the agreed terms.
example: Please issue the invoice as soon as the goods are shipped.
exampleVi: Vui lòng xuất hóa đơn ngay khi hàng được giao.
collocations: issue an invoice, pay an invoice, invoice number, outstanding invoice

definition: The act of receiving, or the fact of having been received.
example: A balance payable on receipt of the goods.
exampleVi: Số dư thanh toán khi nhận hàng.
collocations: keep the receipt, issue a receipt, receipt of payment, sales receipt

definition: An official inspection of a company's financial records to check they are accurate.
example: The finance team is preparing all the documents for the annual audit.
exampleVi: Đội ngũ tài chính đang chuẩn bị tất cả tài liệu cho đợt kiểm toán hàng năm.
collocations: conduct an audit, annual audit, internal audit, audit report

definition: One who audits bookkeeping accounts.
example: An external auditor was hired to review the company's financial statements.
exampleVi: Một kiểm toán viên độc lập đã được thuê để rà soát báo cáo tài chính của công ty.
collocations: external auditor, internal auditor, appoint an auditor, auditor's report

definition: Money paid to the government other than for transaction-specific goods and services.
example: Businesses must file their tax returns before the end of the fiscal year.
exampleVi: Các doanh nghiệp phải nộp tờ khai thuế trước khi kết thúc năm tài chính.
collocations: pay tax, tax return, income tax, tax deduction

definition: Total income or cash flow minus expenditures. The money or other benefit a non-governmental organization or individual receives in exchange for products and services sold at an advertised price.
example: The company reported a record profit for the third quarter.
exampleVi: Công ty báo cáo mức lợi nhuận kỷ lục trong quý ba.
collocations: net profit, gross profit, profit margin, make a profit

definition: The amount by which expenses exceed revenue in a given period.
example: The startup reported a net loss in its first year of operation.
exampleVi: Công ty khởi nghiệp báo cáo lỗ ròng trong năm hoạt động đầu tiên.
collocations: net loss, profit and loss, suffer a loss, loss of revenue

definition: A record of a single financial transaction in an account or ledger.
example: The bookkeeper made an entry for the office supplies purchase.
exampleVi: Nhân viên kế toán đã ghi một bút toán cho việc mua văn phòng phẩm.
collocations: journal entry, make an entry, double entry, entry error

vietnamese: công việc ghi sổ kế toán
definition: The activity of recording a company's day-to-day financial transactions.
example: She handles the bookkeeping for several small businesses.
exampleVi: Cô ấy phụ trách công việc ghi sổ kế toán cho nhiều doanh nghiệp nhỏ.
collocations: double-entry bookkeeping, bookkeeping software, basic bookkeeping

definition: The state of being depreciated; disparagement.
example: The value of the equipment is reduced each year through depreciation.
exampleVi: Giá trị của thiết bị bị giảm mỗi năm do khấu hao.
collocations: asset depreciation, depreciation expense, calculate depreciation

definition: The reduction of loan principal over a series of payments.
example: The loan is repaid through monthly amortization over ten years.
exampleVi: Khoản vay được hoàn trả thông qua việc trả góp hàng tháng trong mười năm.
collocations: loan amortization, amortization schedule, amortize a debt

definition: A formal document summarizing financial information, such as income or bank activity, over a period.
example: Please review the bank statement for any unauthorized charges.
exampleVi: Vui lòng kiểm tra sao kê ngân hàng để phát hiện các khoản phí không được ủy quyền.
collocations: financial statement, bank statement, income statement, issue a statement

definition: The sum of cash revenues and expenditures over a period of time.
example: The company improved its cashflow by collecting payments from clients faster.
exampleVi: Công ty đã cải thiện dòng tiền bằng cách thu tiền từ khách hàng nhanh hơn.
collocations: positive cashflow, cashflow statement, manage cashflow

definition: The process of checking that two sets of financial records, such as a bank statement and a ledger, match.
example: The accountant performs a bank reconciliation at the end of every month.
exampleVi: Kế toán viên thực hiện đối chiếu ngân hàng vào cuối mỗi tháng.
collocations: bank reconciliation, account reconciliation, reconcile the accounts

definition: A diary or daily record of a person, organization, vessel etc.; daybook.
example: Every purchase is first recorded in the accounting journal.
exampleVi: Mọi giao dịch mua hàng đều được ghi vào sổ nhật ký kế toán trước tiên.
collocations: accounting journal, journal entry, sales journal

definition: In bookkeeping, an entry in the left hand column of an account.
example: A cash sale is recorded as debit on the cash account and as credit on the sales account.
exampleVi: Việc bán hàng bằng tiền mặt được ghi nhận là ghi nợ trên tài khoản tiền mặt và ghi có trên tài khoản bán hàng.
collocations: debit card, debit an account, debit entry, direct debit

definition: In bookkeeping, an entry on the right-hand side of an account that increases liabilities or equity.
example: The refund was credited to the customer's account within three days.
exampleVi: Khoản hoàn tiền được ghi có vào tài khoản của khách hàng trong vòng ba ngày.
collocations: credit an account, line of credit, credit entry, credit rating

definition: An increase; something that accumulates, especially an amount of money that periodically accumulates for a specific purpose
example: Under accrual accounting, revenue is recorded when it is earned, not when cash is received.
exampleVi: Theo kế toán dồn tích, doanh thu được ghi nhận khi phát sinh, không phải khi nhận được tiền mặt.
collocations: accrual accounting, accrual basis, interest accrual

definition: Already-produced durable goods available for use as a factor of production, such as steam shovels (equipment) and office buildings (structures).
example: The company raised capital by issuing new shares.
exampleVi: Công ty đã huy động vốn bằng cách phát hành cổ phiếu mới.
collocations: raise capital, working capital, capital investment, capital gains

definition: The degree of which something is in high supply and demand, making it easily convertible to cash
example: My Picasso painting is not very liquid, it would take me months to sell it. Gold on the other hand is convertible to cash at any moment, making it a very liquid commodity.
exampleVi: Bức tranh Picasso của tôi không có tính thanh khoản cao, tôi phải mất nhiều tháng mới bán được nó. Mặt khác, vàng có thể chuyển đổi thành tiền mặt bất cứ lúc nào, khiến nó trở thành một mặt hàng có tính thanh khoản cao.
collocations: liquidity risk, market liquidity, maintain liquidity, liquidity ratio

definition: A payment made by a company to its shareholders out of its profits.
example: The board decided to increase the annual dividend for shareholders.
exampleVi: Hội đồng quản trị quyết định tăng cổ tức hàng năm cho các cổ đông.
collocations: pay a dividend, dividend yield, annual dividend, dividend payment

definition: That which remains when use or need is satisfied, or when a limit is reached; excess; overplus.
example: The government reported a budget surplus for the first time in years.
exampleVi: Chính phủ báo cáo thặng dư ngân sách lần đầu tiên sau nhiều năm.
collocations: budget surplus, trade surplus, surplus funds

definition: Deficiency in amount or quality; a falling short; lack.
example: The company must cut costs to reduce its budget deficit.
exampleVi: Công ty phải cắt giảm chi phí để giảm thâm hụt ngân sách.
collocations: budget deficit, trade deficit, run a deficit

definition: A legally declared or recognized condition of insolvency of a person or organization.
example: The retailer filed for bankruptcy after years of declining sales.
exampleVi: Nhà bán lẻ đã nộp đơn xin phá sản sau nhiều năm doanh số sụt giảm.
collocations: file for bankruptcy, declare bankruptcy, bankruptcy proceedings

definition: A collection of investments, such as stocks and bonds, held by a person or organization.
example: The financial advisor recommended diversifying the client's investment portfolio.
exampleVi: Cố vấn tài chính khuyên khách hàng nên đa dạng hóa danh mục đầu tư.
collocations: investment portfolio, diversify a portfolio, portfolio manager

definition: Act of expending or paying out.
example: The department must justify any expenditure over $5,000.
exampleVi: Bộ phận phải giải trình cho bất kỳ khoản chi tiêu nào trên 5.000 đô la.
collocations: capital expenditure, public expenditure, reduce expenditure

definition: The process or procedure for allocating things, especially money or other resources.
example: The allocation of new permits is on a first-come, first-served basis.
exampleVi: Việc cấp giấy phép mới được thực hiện trên cơ sở ai đến trước được cấp trước.
collocations: budget allocation, resource allocation, allocate funds

definition: The act of varying or the state of being variable.
example: The finance manager investigated the variance between the actual and budgeted costs.
exampleVi: Trưởng phòng tài chính đã kiểm tra sự chênh lệch giữa chi phí thực tế và ngân sách.
collocations: budget variance, cost variance, variance analysis

definition: The state of having enough funds or liquid assets to pay all of one's debts; the state of being solvent.
example: The audit confirmed the company's solvency despite falling sales.
exampleVi: Cuộc kiểm toán xác nhận khả năng thanh toán của công ty dù doanh số giảm.
collocations: financial solvency, solvency ratio, maintain solvency

definition: An estimation of something's worth.
example: An independent expert carried out the valuation of the property.
exampleVi: Một chuyên gia độc lập đã thực hiện việc định giá tài sản.
collocations: company valuation, market valuation, property valuation

definition: The act of compensating someone for an expense.
example: Employees can request reimbursement for approved travel expenses.
exampleVi: Nhân viên có thể yêu cầu hoàn trả các chi phí đi lại đã được phê duyệt.
collocations: expense reimbursement, request reimbursement, reimbursement claim

definition: The act, instance, or process of disbursing.
example: The bank approved the disbursement of the loan in three installments.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng đã phê duyệt việc giải ngân khoản vay thành ba đợt.
collocations: loan disbursement, disbursement schedule, disburse funds

definition: The income return, usually expressed as a percentage, that an investment generates.
example: Government bonds currently offer a yield of about 4%.
exampleVi: Trái phiếu chính phủ hiện có lợi suất khoảng 4%.
collocations: bond yield, dividend yield, annual yield, yield rate

definition: Having enough money to pay all of one's debts.
example: The company remained solvent throughout the economic downturn.
exampleVi: Công ty vẫn có khả năng thanh toán trong suốt thời kỳ suy thoái kinh tế.
collocations: remain solvent, financially solvent, keep a company solvent

definition: A security or guarantee (usually an asset) pledged for the repayment of a loan if one cannot procure enough funds to repay.
example: The bank required the house as collateral for the business loan.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng yêu cầu căn nhà làm tài sản thế chấp cho khoản vay kinh doanh.
collocations: collateral for a loan, pledge collateral, collateral security

definition: The cancellation of an item; the amount cancelled or lost
example: The company recorded a tax write-off for the damaged inventory.
exampleVi: Công ty đã ghi nhận khoản xóa nợ để giảm thuế cho lượng hàng tồn kho bị hư hỏng.
collocations: tax write-off, write off a debt, bad debt write-off

definition: Debts and liabilities owed by a business
example: The finance team tracks accounts payables to make sure suppliers are paid on time.
exampleVi: Đội tài chính theo dõi các khoản phải trả để đảm bảo nhà cung cấp được thanh toán đúng hạn.
collocations: accounts payable, payables balance, manage payables

definition: A debt owed, usually to a business, from the perspective of that business
example: One man's receivable is another man's payable.
exampleVi: Khoản phải thu của người này là khoản phải trả của người khác.
collocations: accounts receivable, collect receivables, receivables turnover

definition: A list of employees who receive salary or wages, together with the amounts due to each.
example: The HR department processes payroll on the last day of each month.
exampleVi: Phòng nhân sự xử lý bảng lương vào ngày cuối cùng của mỗi tháng.
collocations: process payroll, payroll system, payroll tax

definition: The amount of money or resources earmarked for a particular institution, activity or time-frame.
example: The marketing team must stay within its annual budget.
exampleVi: Đội ngũ marketing phải giữ chi tiêu trong ngân sách hàng năm.
collocations: annual budget, budget plan, exceed the budget, tight budget

definition: A person responsible for keeping records or documents, such as of a business.
example: The bookkeeper records all daily transactions in the accounting software.
exampleVi: Nhân viên kế toán ghi lại tất cả giao dịch hàng ngày vào phần mềm kế toán.
collocations: hire a bookkeeper, part-time bookkeeper, bookkeeper's duties

definition: A preliminary check of a company's ledger balances, as in a trial balance.
example: The accountant prepared a trial balance to check that debits equal credits.
exampleVi: Kế toán viên lập bảng cân đối thử để kiểm tra ghi nợ có khớp với ghi có hay không.
collocations: trial balance, trial period, on a trial basis

definition: To transfer a transaction entry into a ledger or account.
example: The clerk posts each day's transactions to the general ledger.
exampleVi: Nhân viên ghi các giao dịch hàng ngày vào sổ cái tổng hợp.
collocations: post a transaction, post an entry, posting date
